briefdescription.para: Transit time delegate injected by the spatial layer.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to zero for benchmark validation. Parameters represent the source machine ID and destination machine ID respectively.

briefdescription.para: Callback for RL integration, invoked when a machine requires a dispatch decision.

detaileddescription.para: The integer parameter passed to the action represents the machine ID that is awaiting a dispatch assignment.

briefdescription.para: True when the simulator is paused waiting for an external dispatch decision.

detaileddescription.para: Set to true inside ProcessNextEvent when TryStartNextOnMachineStepped encounters a non-empty waiting queue. Cleared by ApplyDecision. While true, ProcessNextEvent will immediately return SimStepResult.DecisionRequired without processing further events.

briefdescription.para: The machine ID that requires a dispatch decision.

detaileddescription.para: Only valid when WaitingForDecision is true. The caller should inspect Machines[PendingDecisionMachineId].WaitingQueue to see the candidate operations, then call ApplyDecision.

briefdescription.para: Processes a single event from the queue (stepped execution).

detaileddescription.para: This is the stepped equivalent of the Run loop. Call it repeatedly from the bridge/agent until it returns SimStepResult.Done.
When the return value is SimStepResult.DecisionRequired, the caller MUST call ApplyDecision before calling this method again.

listitem.para: If already waiting for a decision → return DecisionRequired immediately.
If queue is empty → compute makespan, return Done.

Dequeue the next event, advance CurrentTime, and process it.
EventType.JobArrived calls the existing HandleJobArrived (which may auto-start on an idle machine — no decision needed).
EventType.OperationComplete calls HandleOperationCompleteStepped which uses TryStartNextOnMachineStepped instead of the auto-dispatch version.
If the stepped handler flagged a decision → return DecisionRequired.
Otherwise → return Continue or Done depending on remaining events.

simplesect.para: A SimStepResult indicating what the caller should do next.

briefdescription.para: Applies an external dispatch decision and resumes the simulation.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the supplied rule to select the next operation from the pending machine's waiting queue, starts processing it, and enqueues the corresponding EventType.OperationComplete event. Clears the WaitingForDecision flag so ProcessNextEvent can continue.

parameterdescription.para: The DispatchingRule the agent/bridge chose (one of the 8 composite PDRs, or any single rule for validation).

simplesect.para: WaitingForDecision is false, the chosen operation is running on the machine, and a completion event has been enqueued.

briefdescription.para: Loads a Taillard instance and initializes all jobs and machines.

detaileddescription.para: Constructs Machine and Job arrays from the provided instance, mapping raw operation data into typed Operation objects, then calls Reset() to prepare the simulator for execution.

parameterdescription.para: The Taillard benchmark instance to load, containing job count, machine count, and per-job operation sequences with durations.

briefdescription.para: Resets all simulator state without reloading the instance.

detaileddescription.para: Clears the event queue, resets time and statistics counters, restores all machines to MachineState.Idle, clears all machine waiting queues, and re-enqueues a EventType.JobArrived event at t=0 for every job. Call this to re-run the same instance under a different dispatching rule.

briefdescription.para: Runs the full simulation to completion and returns the makespan.

detaileddescription.para: Processes all events in the EventQueue in chronological order, advancing CurrentTime to each event's timestamp and dispatching to either HandleJobArrived or HandleOperationComplete as appropriate. Simulation ends when the queue is empty.

simplesect.para: The makespan, defined as the latest completion time across all jobs.

briefdescription.para: Prints a Gantt-style schedule summary to stdout for debugging.

detaileddescription.para: Outputs the makespan, job and machine counts, total operations completed, and a per-machine timeline showing each operation as [J{jobId} {startTime}-{endTime}], sorted by start time.
Identical to the original except it calls TryStartNextOnMachineStepped, which pauses for an external decision instead of auto-dispatching.

Instead of auto-selecting the next operation via DispatchingRules.SelectNext, this method sets WaitingForDecision and PendingDecisionMachineId, then returns without dispatching. The external caller must inspect the queue and call ApplyDecision with a chosen rule.
If the queue is empty or the machine is not idle, behaves identically to the original (no-op).

parameterdescription.para: The machine that just became free.

detaileddescription.para: Call this at the END of your existing Reset method body, or anywhere after the base reset logic clears the event queue and re-enqueues job arrivals.
Retrieves the arriving job and calls TryDispatchJob if it is not already complete. Early-exits silently if the job has no remaining operations.

parameterdescription.para: The simulation event containing the ID of the arriving job.

detaileddescription.para: Marks the current operation finished on the machine, increments TotalOperationsCompleted, and advances the job's operation index. If the job is now fully complete its Job.CompletionTime is recorded and TotalJobsCompleted is incremented. Otherwise, the transit time to the next machine is queried via GetTransitTime: if zero the job is dispatched immediately, otherwise a future EventType.JobArrived event is enqueued. Finally, TryStartNextOnMachine is called to assign a waiting job to the newly freed machine.

parameterdescription.para: The simulation event containing the completing job ID and machine ID.

briefdescription.para: Attempts to assign a job's current operation to its required machine.

detaileddescription.para: If the target machine is MachineState.Idle the operation starts immediately and a corresponding EventType.OperationComplete event is enqueued. If the machine is busy the operation is appended to the machine's Machine.WaitingQueue to be picked up when the machine next becomes free.

parameterdescription.para: The job whose Job.CurrentOperation should be dispatched.

briefdescription.para: Selects and starts the next operation from a machine's waiting queue.

detaileddescription.para: No-ops if the queue is empty or the machine is not yet idle. Otherwise delegates selection to DispatchingRules.SelectNext using ActiveRule, removes the chosen operation from the queue, starts processing, and enqueues the resulting EventType.OperationComplete event.

parameterdescription.para: The machine that has just become free and requires a new assignment.

briefdescription.para: Pure discrete-event simulator for Job Shop Problem (JSP) validation.

detaileddescription.para: No Unity dependencies — can run standalone or inside Unity. Transit times are zero for benchmark validation. The RL layer will later wrap this and inject non-zero transit via AGVs.
